data.text/markdown: First, let's remove all the columns that have more than 60% of their values missing. These columns are mostly useless for the purposes of this data analysis project or have too much missing that it is not worth keeping or trying to impute.

data.text/markdown: Now let's remove buyer and listing agent related columns since we care about real estate, not the agents.

console.name: stdout
console.text:
Removing 9 agent columns from listings:
['ListAgentEmail', 'ListAgentFirstName', 'ListAgentFullName', 'ListAgentLastName', 'ListOfficeName', 'ListingKey', 'Unnamed: 0', 'ListingKeyNumeric', 'ListingId']

 Index(['OriginalListPrice', 'Latitude', 'Longitude', 'UnparsedAddress', 'PropertyType', 'LivingArea', 'ListPrice', 'DaysOnMarket', 'AssociationFeeFrequency', 'MLSAreaMajor', 'CountyOrParish',
       'MlsStatus', 'AttachedGarageYN', 'ParkingTotal', 'PropertySubType', 'LotSizeAcres', 'YearBuilt', 'StreetNumberNumeric', 'BathroomsTotalInteger', 'City', 'BedroomsTotal',
       'ContractStatusChangeDate', 'PurchaseContractDate', 'ListingContractDate', 'StateOrProvince', 'FireplaceYN', 'Stories', 'Levels', 'LotSizeArea', 'MainLevelBedrooms', 'NewConstructionYN',
       'GarageSpaces', 'HighSchoolDistrict', 'PostalCode', 'AssociationFee', 'LotSizeSquareFeet', 'year_month', 'rate_30yr_fixed'],
      dtype='str')
